title: quercus cepifera
intro: Quercus cepifera, also known as the Japanese evergreen oak, is a species of deciduous tree native to Japan. The leaves, bark, and other parts of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Quercus cepifera include reducing fever and relieving symptoms of gastrointestinal disorders, as well as its potential to improve cardiovascular health. Quercus cepifera is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
